introduction: Today, we'll be discussing our customers' usage of in-store self-checkout kiosks in Q4 2024. This analysis will provide insights into our customer behavior and their interaction with our technology.

subsections.heading: Overview of In-Store Technology Usage
subsections.content:
• In Q4, there was a 12% rise in the use of our mobile app for in-store navigation
• The number of customers using in-store WiFi saw a 15% increase over the quarter
• The digital coupon usage increased by 20% compared to the same period last year
• In-store digital signage influenced 30% of impulse purchases during the holiday season
• Contactless payment options were utilized in 65% of transactions in Q4
• The 'Scan & Go' feature was used by 22% of customers, showing a slight increase compared to Q3
subsections.justification: The content focuses on various in-store technology usage, not specifically on self-checkout kiosks.

subsections.heading: Detailed Analysis of Self-Checkout Kiosk Interactions
subsections.content:
• The average transaction time at self-checkout kiosks was 3 minutes and 15 seconds
• Approximately 18% of total transactions were conducted via self-checkout kiosks
• There was a 5% decrease in self-checkout kiosk usage during peak hours
• Analysis shows a slight preference for self-checkout among customers aged 25-35
• The number of items per transaction at self-checkout kiosks averaged at 12
• The error rate at self-checkout kiosks remained steady at around 2% throughout Q4
subsections.justification: The slide provides a general overview of self-checkout kiosk usage, but does not reveal the total customer count for Q4 2024.

subsections.heading: Comparison with Traditional Checkout Metrics
subsections.content:
• Traditional checkouts handled 82% of total transactions in Q4
• Average transaction time at traditional checkouts was 5 minutes and 30 seconds
• The item count per transaction at traditional checkouts averaged at 20
• Customer satisfaction scores were marginally higher for traditional checkouts
• During peak hours, traditional checkouts processed 90% of transactions
• Customers aged over 45 showed a strong preference for traditional checkouts
subsections.justification: The content focuses on traditional checkout metrics, and does not provide information on self-checkout kiosk customer numbers.

conclusion: In conclusion, our self-checkout kiosks have been well-utilized by our customers. This usage data can inform further investment in technology to improve the shopping experience at Lee's Market.

image_queries.slide_title: Detailed Analysis of Self-Checkout Kiosk Interactions
